# validate.reliability_curve { #tspaint.validate.reliability_curve }

```python
tspaint.validate.reliability_curve(
    tracks,
    truth_states,
    state=0,
    n_bins=10,
    exclude_missing=True,
)
```

Calibration curve of ``P(state)``: predicted vs. empirical per bin.

A well-calibrated painter has ``pred ≈ emp`` in every populated bin.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**tracks**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[Segment](`tspaint.output.Segment`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Posterior segment tracks per sample.

<code>[**truth_states**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   True ancestry-state tracts per sample (e.g. from :func:`map_truth`).

<code>[**state**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Ancestry-state index whose posterior probability is binned.

<code>[**n_bins**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of equal-width probability bins on ``[0, 1]``.

<code>[**exclude_missing**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If True, skip segments tagged :data:`tspaint.output.MISSING_INFO`.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Keys ``"pred"``, ``"emp"`` and ``"weight"``, each a 1-D array over the populated bins giving the span-weighted mean predicted probability, the empirical frequency of ``state``, and the total span weight respectively.
